info.name: Stock Prices
info.description:
REST endpoints for the price-alert system: alert-rule-service (CRUD for alert rules) and notification-service (read-only notification history + STOMP broadcast, not covered here since Postman doesn't speak STOMP). price-ingestion-service and alert-evaluation-service expose no REST API — they're Kafka-only.

Run against a local `docker compose up` stack. See CLAUDE.md for build/run instructions.

request.description:
Creates an active alert rule with no cooldown yet applied. condition is one of PRICE_ABOVE / PRICE_BELOW. Sets the collectionVariable `ruleId` from the response for use by the requests below.

A threshold of 1 for a PRICE_ABOVE BTC rule will fire on the very next price tick (real BTC price is always above 1), handy for end-to-end testing.
Flips the `active` flag. Unrelated to the evaluation cooldown, which is purely time-based (lastTriggeredAt + 15m by default) and never touches this flag.

Read-only history, ordered newest first. Rows only appear once alert-evaluation-service has actually fired a rule for this user (see the Create Alert Rule request in the alert-rule-service folder, and give it ~45s for the next price tick to land).

For the live push side of this service, see the "STOMP Broadcast" WebSocket request below.

Postman's collection JSON format doesn't reliably round-trip a WebSocket request's message history on import (it's a newer, less formalized feature than HTTP requests), so this may open as a plain request in your client. If so: right-click this request -> "Edit" -> change the request type to WebSocket Request (or just create a new WebSocket Request and paste in this URL: ws://localhost:8082/ws).

This endpoint is registered with SockJS fallback (see WebSocketConfig), but Spring's SockJS handler also accepts a direct raw WebSocket upgrade at the bare endpoint path (no /info negotiation needed) - which is exactly what connecting straight to ws://localhost:8082/ws does. Once connected, send these two raw STOMP frames in order, each as a separate message, EXACTLY as shown including the blank line and the trailing NUL byte (STOMP frames are NUL-terminated, not newline-terminated - if your Postman version's WebSocket composer won't let you type \u0000, try its "hex"/raw mode and append byte 00, or use a Node/wscat/websocat client instead):

1) CONNECT\naccept-version:1.2\nhost:localhost\n\n\u0000

2) SUBSCRIBE\nid:0\ndestination:/topic/notifications\n\n\u0000

After step 1 you should receive a CONNECTED frame back. After step 2, trigger a notification (run "Create Alert Rule" in the alert-rule-service folder, then wait ~45s for the next price tick) and watch this connection for an incoming MESSAGE frame containing the NotificationPayload JSON.
